title: 🎙️ Indigenous Lands & Innovation
description: Today we explore how Cariboo and BC Indigenous nations are using technology on their own terms — for language revitalization, land stewardship, cultural preservation, and self-determined governance. We're looking for stories that centre Indigenous voices and leadership in innovation, where technology serves community sovereignty rather than replacing it. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and BC Indigenous nations are using technology on their own terms — for language revitalization, land stewardship, cultural preservation, and self-determined governance. We're looking for stories that centre Indigenous voices and leadership in innovation, where technology serves community sovereignty rather than replacing it.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Indigenous Lands & Innovation' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: language revitalization technology for Tŝilhqot'in/Secwépemc/Dakelh, Indigenous guardian programs using remote monitoring and sensors, land management tech and environmental monitoring on Indigenous lands, digital tools for traditional knowledge preservation, community mapping and GIS for land claims, Indigenous-led connectivity projects, technology for cultural heritage documentation, mobile apps for language learning, drone/satellite monitoring for stewardship, reconciliation through digital collaboration, and how Cariboo Indigenous nations use innovation for self-determination. Prefer articles that bridge Indigenous governance/culture AND technology. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [New Atlas] This cargo ebike could replace your family car but fits in your closet
items.content_html:
Most electric cargo bikes live in garages, because that's the only place big enough to put them. JackRabbit's new MG Cargo is so slim it can live in a closet or a hallway. It will still carry your kids, a week's worth of groceries, or an adult passenger on the back – all on a frame that weighs less than a suitcase.

Continue Reading

Category: Urban Transport, Transport

Tags: ebikes, cargo bike, JackRabbit

items.title: [New Atlas] Antarctic telescope spies alien worlds with ever-shifting orbits
items.content_html:
Planetary systems evolve over dizzying timespans. It typically takes millions, even billions, of years for gravitational interactions to noticeably alter a planet’s orbital trajectory. This stability allowed life to take hold on Earth. It also means orbital planes are effectively fixed on human timescales.

Continue Reading

Category: Astronomy, Science

Tags: Exoplanet, Antarctic, Orbit

items.title: [Boing Boing] Antarctic detector captured 13 radio pulses from cosmic rays in ice
items.content_html:
Neutrinos pass through the entire planet without stopping. They travel billions of light-years from colliding black holes and exploding stars without being absorbed or deflected, arriving intact and pointing straight back at their source. But catching enough of them to learn anything requires a detector the size of a city. — Read the rest

The post Antarctic detector captured 13 radio pulses from cosmic rays in ice appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [New Atlas] 'Metajets' could allow future spaceships to be propelled by nothing but light
items.content_html:
If an alien in the Alpha Centauri star system were craving pizza, it would take tens of thousands of years to deliver it using today’s rocket technology. According to a press release, researchers at Texas A&M University have developed a technology that could one day reduce delivery to a mere 20 years using nothing but light for propulsion.

Continue Reading

Category: Space Systems, Engineering

Tags: Texas A&M Universty, Solar Sail
